chunks.text: Eric Paulos elected to the ACM CHI Academy - EECS at Berkeley The Association for Computing Machinery’s Special Interest Group on Computer-Human Interaction (ACM SIGCHI) has elected Eric Paulos, Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences, to the ACM CHI Academy. Paulos is one of eleven individuals inducted to the Academy this year. The CHI Academy is an honorary group recognizing individuals who have made substantial contributions to human-computer interaction and whose work has shaped the discipline and advanced research and innovation in interactive technologies. Paulos is recognized for his work exploring how people interact with networked devices, sensing systems, and urban technologies. His research spans human-computer interaction, design, and emerging technologies, of
